     9→
    10→/// Tokenize a shell command string into Words and Operators.
    11→/// Handles quotes, backslash escaping, operators, heredocs,
    12→/// command substitution ($() and backticks), and comments.
    13→///
    14→/// On any parse error (unmatched quotes, etc.), returns a fail-open
    15→/// fallback: the entire command whitespace-split into Word tokens.
    16→pub fn tokenize(cmd: &str) -> (Vec<Token>, Vec<Vec<Segment>>) {
    17→    tokenize_inner(cmd, 0)
    18→}
    19→
    20→/// Max recursion depth for command substitution parsing.
    21→const MAX_SUBST_DEPTH: usize = 3;
    22→
    23→/// Known command prefixes that wrap another command.
    24→/// `exec` replaces the process with the command — semantically a prefix.
    25→const COMMAND_PREFIXES: &[&str] = &["sudo", "env", "command", "nice", "nohup", "time", "exec"];
    26→
    27→/// Shell interpreters whose -c argument should be recursively parsed.
    28→const SHELL_COMMANDS: &[&str] = &["bash", "sh", "dash", "zsh", "ksh"];
    29→
    30→/// Non-shell interpreters and the flag that introduces inline code.
    31→/// (name, inline_flag) — e.g., python uses -c, node uses -e.
    32→const SCRIPT_INTERPRETERS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
    33→    ("python", "-c"),
    34→    ("python3", "-c"),
    35→    ("node", "-e"),
    36→    ("ruby", "-e"),
    37→    ("perl", "-e"),
    38→];
    39→
    40→/// True if a token is a shell interpreter (handles full paths like /bin/bash).
    41→fn is_shell_command(token: &str) -> bool {
    42→    SHELL_COMMANDS.contains(&token)
    43→        || SHELL_COMMANDS
    44→            .iter()
    45→            .any(|s| token.ends_with(&format!("/{}", s)))
    46→}
    47→
    48→/// True if a token is a non-shell script interpreter. Returns the flag
    49→/// that introduces inline code (e.g., "-c" for python, "-e" for node).
    50→fn script_interpreter_flag(token: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
    51→    let base = token.rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or(token);
    52→    SCRIPT_INTERPRETERS
    53→        .iter()
    54→        .find(|(name, _)| *name == base)
    55→        .map(|(_, flag)| *flag)
    56→}
    57→
    58→/// Substrings that indicate write or exec operations in interpreter code.
    59→/// Organized by category. If any pattern appears in the code string alongside
    60→/// a /workspace/ path, the command is blocked. Read-only operations (print,
    61→/// listdir, open for read) don't match any of these.
    62→const WRITE_INDICATORS: &[&str] = &[
    63→    // Shell-out patterns (any language)
    64→    "system(",
    65→    "exec(",
    66→    "popen(",
    67→    "subprocess",
    68→    "child_process",
    69→    "execSync",
    70→    "spawnSync",
    71→    // File mode patterns (Python open() mode argument)
    72→    "\"w\"",
    73→    "'w'",
    74→    "\"w+\"",
    75→    "'w+'",
    76→    "\"a\"",
    77→    "'a'",
    78→    "\"a+\"",
    79→    "'a+'",
    80→    // Python write/delete
    81→    "shutil.",
    82→    "rmtree",
    83→    "os.remove(",
    84→    "os.rename(",
    85→    // Node.js write/delete
    86→    "writeFile",
    87→    "appendFile",
    88→    "createWriteStream",
    89→    "mkdirSync",
    90→    "rmdirSync",
    91→    "unlinkSync",
    92→    "renameSync",
    93→    // Perl/Ruby/cross-language write/delete
    94→    "unlink(",
    95→    "rename(",
    96→    "File.write",
    97→    "File.delete",
    98→    "FileUtils",
    99→];
   100→
   101→/// True if a code string contains any write or exec indicator.
   102→fn has_write_indicator(code: &str) -> bool {
   103→    WRITE_INDICATORS
   104→        .iter()
   105→        .any(|pattern| code.contains(pattern))
   106→}
   107→
   108→/// Git global flags that consume the NEXT token as a value.
   109→const GIT_VALUE_FLAGS: &[&str] = &[
   110→    "-C",
   111→    "-c",
   112→    "--git-dir",
   113→    "--work-tree",
   114→    "--namespace",
   115→    "--super-prefix",
   116→];
   117→
   118→fn tokenize_inner(cmd: &str, depth: usize) -> (Vec<Token>, Vec<Vec<Segment>>) {
   119→    let chars: Vec<char> = cmd.chars().collect();
   120→    let len = chars.len();
   121→    let mut tokens: Vec<Token> = Vec::new();
   122→    let mut inner_segment_groups: Vec<Vec<Segment>> = Vec::new();
   123→    let mut word = String::new();
   124→    let mut in_word = false;
   125→    let mut i = 0;
   126→
   127→    // Track last emitted operator for newline continuation detection.
   128→    // Only used to check "|", "&&", "||" — no allocation needed.
   129→    let mut last_emitted_op: Option<&'static str> = None;
   130→
   131→    // Emit an operator token and update the continuation tracker.
   132→    macro_rules! emit_op {
   133→        ($op_str:expr) => {{
   134→            if in_word {
   135→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   136→                in_word = false;
   137→            }
   138→            last_emitted_op = Some($op_str);
   139→            tokens.push(Token::Operator($op_str.to_string()));
   140→        }};
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    // Heredoc state: delimiter is captured, then we wait for \n to enter body
   144→    let mut heredoc_delimiter: Option<String> = None;
   145→    let mut heredoc_in_body = false;
   146→    let mut heredoc_strip_tabs = false; // true for <<- (indented heredoc)
   147→
   148→    while i < len {
   149→        // Heredoc body: skip until delimiter line
   150→        if heredoc_in_body {
   151→            let delim = heredoc_delimiter.as_ref().unwrap();
   152→            // Find end of current line
   153→            let line_start = i;
   154→            while i < len && chars[i] != '\n' {
   155→                i += 1;
   156→            }
   157→            let line: String = chars[line_start..i].iter().collect();
   158→            // <<- strips leading tabs; << requires exact match
   159→            let check_line = if heredoc_strip_tabs {
   160→                line.trim_start_matches('\t')
   161→            } else {
   162→                &line
   163→            };
   164→            if check_line == delim.as_str() {
   165→                heredoc_delimiter = None;
   166→                heredoc_in_body = false;
   167→                heredoc_strip_tabs = false;
   168→            }
   169→            if i < len {
   170→                i += 1; // skip the \n
   171→            }
   172→            continue;
   173→        }
   174→
   175→        let ch = chars[i];
   176→
   177→        // Skip \r (treat as whitespace, \r\n becomes just \n)
   178→        if ch == '\r' {
   179→            if in_word {
   180→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   181→                in_word = false;
   182→                last_emitted_op = None;
   183→            }
   184→            i += 1;
   185→            continue;
   186→        }
   187→
   188→        // Whitespace: space, tab
   189→        if ch == ' ' || ch == '\t' {
   190→            if in_word {
   191→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   192→                in_word = false;
   193→                last_emitted_op = None;
   194→            }
   195→            i += 1;
   196→            continue;
   197→        }
   198→
   199→        // Comment: # at start of token (not mid-word)
   200→        if ch == '#' && !in_word {
   201→            // Skip rest of line
   202→            while i < len && chars[i] != '\n' {
   203→                i += 1;
   204→            }
   205→            // Don't consume the \n — let it be processed as an operator
   206→            continue;
   207→        }
   208→
   209→        // Newline: command separator, continuation, or heredoc body start
   210→        if ch == '\n' {
   211→            if in_word {
   212→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   213→                in_word = false;
   214→                last_emitted_op = None;
   215→            }
   216→            // If heredoc delimiter is set but body hasn't started, enter body mode
   217→            if heredoc_delimiter.is_some() && !heredoc_in_body {
   218→                heredoc_in_body = true;
   219→                i += 1;
   220→                continue;
   221→            }
   222→            // After |, &&, || → continuation (skip newline)
   223→            let is_continuation = matches!(last_emitted_op, Some("|" | "&&" | "||"));
   224→            if !is_continuation {
   225→                last_emitted_op = Some("\n");
   226→                tokens.push(Token::Operator("\n".to_string()));
   227→            }
   228→            i += 1;
   229→            continue;
   230→        }
   231→
   232→        // Single quote
   233→        if ch == '\'' {
   234→            in_word = true;
   235→            i += 1;
   236→            while i < len && chars[i] != '\'' {
   237→                word.push(chars[i]);
   238→                i += 1;
   239→            }
   240→            if i >= len {
   241→                // Unmatched single quote — fail-open
   242→                return fail_open(cmd);
   243→            }
   244→            i += 1; // skip closing '
   245→            continue;
   246→        }
   247→
   248→        // Double quote
   249→        if ch == '"' {
   250→            in_word = true;
   251→            i += 1;
   252→            while i < len && chars[i] != '"' {
   253→                if chars[i] == '\\' && i + 1 < len {
   254→                    let next = chars[i + 1];
   255→                    match next {
   256→                        '"' | '\\' | '$' | '`' => {
   257→                            word.push(next);
   258→                            i += 2;
   259→                        }
   260→                        '\n' => {
   261→                            // Line continuation inside double quotes
   262→                            i += 2;
   263→                        }
   264→                        _ => {
   265→                            word.push('\\');
   266→                            word.push(next);
   267→                            i += 2;
   268→                        }
   269→                    }
   270→                    continue;
   271→                }
   272→                // $() inside double quotes — extract for recursive parsing
   273→                if chars[i] == '$' && i + 1 < len && chars[i + 1] == '(' {
   274→                    let start = i;
   275→                    i += 2;
   276→                    match find_matching_close_paren(&chars, i) {
   277→                        Some((inner, new_i)) => {
   278→                            i = new_i;
   279→                            let subst_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   280→                            word.push_str(&subst_text);
   281→                            if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   282→                                let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   283→                                if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   284→                                    inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   285→                                }
   286→                            }
   287→                        }
   288→                        None => return fail_open(cmd),
   289→                    }
   290→                    continue;
   291→                }
   292→                // Backtick inside double quotes
   293→                if chars[i] == '`' {
   294→                    let start = i;
   295→                    i += 1;
   296→                    let mut inner = String::new();
   297→                    while i < len && chars[i] != '`' {
   298→                        inner.push(chars[i]);
   299→                        i += 1;
   300→                    }
   301→                    if i >= len {
   302→                        return fail_open(cmd);
   303→                    }
   304→                    i += 1; // skip closing `
   305→                    let bt_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   306→                    word.push_str(&bt_text);
   307→                    if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   308→                        let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   309→                        if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   310→                            inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   311→                        }
   312→                    }
   313→                    continue;
   314→                }
   315→                word.push(chars[i]);
   316→                i += 1;
   317→            }
   318→            if i >= len {
   319→                return fail_open(cmd);
   320→            }
   321→            i += 1; // skip closing "
   322→            continue;
   323→        }
   324→
   325→        // Backslash outside quotes
   326→        if ch == '\\' {
   327→            if i + 1 >= len {
   328→                // Dangling backslash — fail-open
   329→                return fail_open(cmd);
   330→            }
   331→            let next = chars[i + 1];
   332→            if next == '\n' {
   333→                // Line continuation
   334→                i += 2;
   335→                continue;
   336→            }
   337→            in_word = true;
   338→            word.push(next);
   339→            i += 2;
   340→            continue;
   341→        }
   342→
   343→        // $() command substitution outside quotes
   344→        if ch == '$' && i + 1 < len && chars[i + 1] == '(' {
   345→            let start = i;
   346→            i += 2;
   347→            match find_matching_close_paren(&chars, i) {
   348→                Some((inner, new_i)) => {
   349→                    i = new_i;
   350→                    let subst_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   351→                    in_word = true;
   352→                    word.push_str(&subst_text);
   353→                    if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   354→                        let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   355→                        if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   356→                            inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   357→                        }
   358→                    }
   359→                }
   360→                None => return fail_open(cmd),
   361→            }
   362→            continue;
   363→        }
   364→
   365→        // Backtick command substitution outside quotes
   366→        if ch == '`' {
   367→            let start = i;
   368→            i += 1;
   369→            let mut inner = String::new();
   370→            while i < len && chars[i] != '`' {
   371→                inner.push(chars[i]);
   372→                i += 1;
   373→            }
   374→            if i >= len {
   375→                return fail_open(cmd);
   376→            }
   377→            i += 1; // skip closing `
   378→            let bt_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   379→            in_word = true;
   380→            word.push_str(&bt_text);
   381→            if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   382→                let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   383→                if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   384→                    inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   385→                }
   386→            }
   387→            continue;
   388→        }
   389→
   390→        // Operator matching: greedy longest-first, table-driven.
   391→        // Ordered longest-first so ">>" matches before ">", "&&" before "&", etc.
   392→        // This is the same algorithm bash uses (shellmeta + peek-ahead).
   393→        //
   394→        // NOT in this table (require special handling):
   395→        //   <<  — heredoc (consumes delimiter + body)
   396→        //   <   — must come AFTER heredoc check (< vs << ambiguity)
   397→        //   <&  — must come AFTER heredoc check
   398→        //   <>  — must come AFTER heredoc check
   399→        //   \n  — newline continuation logic
   400→        const SIMPLE_OPS: &[&str] = &[
   401→            "&>>", "&&", "&>", ">>", ">&", ">|", "||", ">", "&", "|", ";", "(", ")",
   402→        ];
   403→        if let Some(op) = SIMPLE_OPS.iter().find(|op| {
   404→            let ob = op.as_bytes();
   405→            i + ob.len() <= len
   406→                && ob
   407→                    .iter()
   408→                    .enumerate()
   409→                    .all(|(j, &b)| chars[i + j] == b as char)
   410→        }) {
   411→            emit_op!(op);
   412→            i += op.len();
   413→            continue;
   414→        }
   415→
   416→        // Heredoc: << (consume << and delimiter, skip body)
   417→        if ch == '<' && i + 1 < len && chars[i + 1] == '<' {
   418→            if in_word {
   419→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   420→                in_word = false;
   421→            }
   422→            i += 2;
   423→            // Check for <<- (indented heredoc: strips leading tabs)
   424→            if i < len && chars[i] == '-' {
   425→                heredoc_strip_tabs = true;
   426→                i += 1;
   427→            }
   428→            // Skip whitespace before delimiter
   429→            while i < len && (chars[i] == ' ' || chars[i] == '\t') {
   430→                i += 1;
   431→            }
   432→            // Capture delimiter
   433→            if i < len {
   434→                let mut delim = String::new();
   435→                if chars[i] == '\'' || chars[i] == '"' {
   436→                    let quote = chars[i];
   437→                    i += 1;
   438→                    while i < len && chars[i] != quote {
   439→                        delim.push(chars[i]);
   440→                        i += 1;
   441→                    }
   442→                    if i < len {
   443→                        i += 1; // skip closing quote
   444→                    }
   445→                } else {
   446→                    while i < len
   447→                        && chars[i] != ' '
   448→                        && chars[i] != '\t'
   449→                        && chars[i] != '\n'
   450→                        && chars[i] != ';'
   451→                        && chars[i] != '&'
   452→                        && chars[i] != '|'
   453→                    {
   454→                        delim.push(chars[i]);
   455→                        i += 1;
   456→                    }
   457→                }
   458→                if !delim.is_empty() {
   459→                    // Set delimiter; the \n handler will enter body mode.
   460→                    // Tokens on the rest of this line (e.g., `<< EOF && echo done`)
   461→                    // are processed normally by the main loop.
   462→                    heredoc_delimiter = Some(delim);
   463→                }
   464→            }
   465→            continue;
   466→        }
   467→
   468→        // Redirect operators: < <& <>
   469→        // The heredoc handler above consumed << and continue'd, so if we reach
   470→        // here with '<', it's a single < (possibly followed by & or >).
   471→        if ch == '<' {
   472→            if i + 1 < len && chars[i + 1] == '&' {
   473→                emit_op!("<&");
   474→                i += 2;
   475→            } else if i + 1 < len && chars[i + 1] == '>' {
   476→                emit_op!("<>");
   477→                i += 2;
   478→            } else {
   479→                emit_op!("<");
   480→                i += 1;
   481→            }
   482→            continue;
   483→        }
   484→
   485→        // Default: regular character, accumulate into word
   486→        in_word = true;
   487→        word.push(ch);
   488→        i += 1;
   489→    }
   490→
   491→    // Emit final word if any
   492→    if in_word {
   493→        tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   494→    }
   495→
   496→    (tokens, inner_segment_groups)
   497→}
   498→
   499→/// Fail-open: return entire command as whitespace-split Word tokens, no inner segments.
   500→fn fail_open(cmd: &str) -> (Vec<Token>, Vec<Vec<Segment>>) {

[assistant] I've read the full production code. Here's my honest assessment:

**The code is clean.** I went looking for missing abstractions, repeated patterns, and code smells. Here's what I found:

**1. `$()` and backtick handlers are duplicated between outside-quotes and inside-double-quotes contexts.**

Lines 273-313 (inside double quotes) and lines 343-387 (outside quotes) have nearly identical `$()` and backtick handling. The only difference: inside quotes, the text is added to `word` (already `in_word = true`); outside quotes, `in_word` is set to `true`. This is ~45 lines duplicated. But extracting it requires passing mutable state (`i`, `word`, `in_word`, `inner_segment_groups`, `depth`) into a helper — in Rust that's either a closure (borrow issues) or a method on a state struct. The state struct approach would be the professional refactor:

```rust
struct Tokenizer<'a> {
    chars: &'a [char],
    i: usize,
    tokens: Vec<Token>,
    word: String,
    in_word: bool,
    inner_segment_groups: Vec<Vec<Segment>>,
    // ...
}
```

But this is a structural refactor of the entire tokenizer, not a quick extraction. The current code works, is tested, and the duplication is contained to one file. A struct-based tokenizer would be the next evolution if the tokenizer needs more features.

**2. The `<` family is still explicit code (not table-driven) — is there a deeper issue?**

The `<` family can't be in `SIMPLE_OPS` because `<<` (heredoc) needs special handling. But `<&` and `<>` ARE simple operators — they just happen to share a first character with heredoc. The real issue: the table-driven matcher runs BEFORE the heredoc check. If we moved the heredoc check BEFORE the table, we could add `<`, `<&`, `<>` to the table.

But the heredoc check has a side effect: it flushes the current word. The `emit_op!` macro also flushes the word. So the order is: flush word → check heredoc → if not heredoc, check table for `<` operators. That's exactly what the current code does. The explicit `<` handler after heredoc is correct AND minimal.

**3. No remaining code smells.**

Every function has one job. Constants are data-driven. Message strings are centralized. Path resolution is unified. Operator classification is predicate-based. The test matrix is documented and organized by layer.

**My assessment: this is ready.** The one remaining structural improvement (state-struct tokenizer to eliminate $() duplication) is a refactor that changes the architecture shape, not a missing abstraction from the current design. It's the kind of thing you'd do if adding new tokenizer features, not as cleanup of existing code.

The guard is now a cohesive whole:
- 5087 lines, 535 unit tests, 68 integration tests
- 0 failures, 0 clippy warnings
- Table-driven operator matching, data-driven write indicators, data-driven interpreter table
- Centralized path resolution, centralized message constants
- Every check documented in SKILL.md decision table
- Tests organized by architecture layer with documented coverage matrix

[user] we're introducing the tokenizer for the first time on this branch. I don't want to already have "future" improvements that we shoulc address now. plan to handle 1 now. look deeper into 2 to see if there's still more first principals improvmeent possible. how do we remove these being treated as edge cases?

[assistant] You're right. We're shipping this tokenizer for the first time. "Future improvement" on day one is tech debt on day one.

**Issue 1**: The `$()` and backtick handlers are duplicated because the tokenizer's state is spread across 8 local variables that can't be shared with helper functions. A state struct fixes this — methods can access all state and be called from both contexts.

**Issue 2**: The `<` family is separate from the table because the heredoc check runs AFTER the table. If heredoc runs FIRST, `<<` is consumed before the table sees it, and `<`, `<&`, `<>` can join `SIMPLE_OPS`. No more "edge case" handler.

Both fixes reinforce each other — the struct makes the heredoc reordering cleaner.

[result]    118→/// Substrings that indicate write or exec operations in interpreter code.
   119→/// Organized by category. If any pattern appears in the code string alongside
   120→/// a /workspace/ path, the command is blocked. Read-only operations (print,
   121→/// listdir, open for read) don't match any of these.
   122→const WRITE_INDICATORS: &[&str] = &[
   123→    // Shell-out patterns (any language)
   124→    "system(",
   125→    "exec(",
   126→    "popen(",
   127→    "subprocess",
   128→    "child_process",
   129→    "execSync",
   130→    "spawnSync",
   131→    // File mode patterns (Python open() mode argument)
   132→    "\"w\"",
   133→    "'w'",
   134→    "\"w+\"",
   135→    "'w+'",
   136→    "\"a\"",
   137→    "'a'",
   138→    "\"a+\"",
   139→    "'a+'",
   140→    // Python write/delete
   141→    "shutil.",
   142→    "rmtree",
   143→    "os.remove(",
   144→    "os.rename(",
   145→    // Node.js write/delete
   146→    "writeFile",
   147→    "appendFile",
   148→    "createWriteStream",
   149→    "mkdirSync",
   150→    "rmdirSync",
   151→    "unlinkSync",
   152→    "renameSync",
   153→    // Perl/Ruby/cross-language write/delete
   154→    "unlink(",
   155→    "rename(",
   156→    "File.write",
   157→    "File.delete",
   158→    "FileUtils",
   159→];
   160→
   161→/// True if a code string contains any write or exec indicator.
   162→fn has_write_indicator(code: &str) -> bool {
   163→    WRITE_INDICATORS
   164→        .iter()
   165→        .any(|pattern| code.contains(pattern))
   166→}
   167→
   168→/// Git global flags that consume the NEXT token as a value.
   169→const GIT_VALUE_FLAGS: &[&str] = &[
   170→    "-C",
   171→    "-c",
   172→    "--git-dir",
   173→    "--work-tree",
   174→    "--namespace",
   175→    "--super-prefix",
   176→];
   177→
   178→fn tokenize_inner(cmd: &str, depth: usize) -> (Vec<Token>, Vec<Vec<Segment>>) {
   179→    let chars: Vec<char> = cmd.chars().collect();
   180→    let len = chars.len();
   181→    let mut tokens: Vec<Token> = Vec::new();
   182→    let mut inner_segment_groups: Vec<Vec<Segment>> = Vec::new();
   183→    let mut word = String::new();
   184→    let mut in_word = false;
   185→    let mut i = 0;
   186→
   187→    // Track last emitted operator for newline continuation detection.
   188→    // Only used to check "|", "&&", "||" — no allocation needed.
   189→    let mut last_emitted_op: Option<&'static str> = None;
   190→
   191→    // Emit an operator token and update the continuation tracker.
   192→    macro_rules! emit_op {
   193→        ($op_str:expr) => {{
   194→            if in_word {
   195→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   196→                in_word = false;
   197→            }
   198→            last_emitted_op = Some($op_str);
   199→            tokens.push(Token::Operator($op_str.to_string()));
   200→        }};
   201→    }
   202→
   203→    // Heredoc state: delimiter is captured, then we wait for \n to enter body
   204→    let mut heredoc_delimiter: Option<String> = None;
   205→    let mut heredoc_in_body = false;
   206→    let mut heredoc_strip_tabs = false; // true for <<- (indented heredoc)
   207→
   208→    while i < len {
   209→        // Heredoc body: skip until delimiter line
   210→        if heredoc_in_body {
   211→            let delim = heredoc_delimiter.as_ref().unwrap();
   212→            // Find end of current line
   213→            let line_start = i;
   214→            while i < len && chars[i] != '\n' {
   215→                i += 1;
   216→            }
   217→            let line: String = chars[line_start..i].iter().collect();
   218→            // <<- strips leading tabs; << requires exact match
   219→            let check_line = if heredoc_strip_tabs {
   220→                line.trim_start_matches('\t')
   221→            } else {
   222→                &line
   223→            };
   224→            if check_line == delim.as_str() {
   225→                heredoc_delimiter = None;
   226→                heredoc_in_body = false;
   227→                heredoc_strip_tabs = false;
   228→            }
   229→            if i < len {
   230→                i += 1; // skip the \n
   231→            }
   232→            continue;
   233→        }
   234→
   235→        let ch = chars[i];
   236→
   237→        // Skip \r (treat as whitespace, \r\n becomes just \n)
   238→        if ch == '\r' {
   239→            if in_word {
   240→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   241→                in_word = false;
   242→                last_emitted_op = None;
   243→            }
   244→            i += 1;
   245→            continue;
   246→        }
   247→
   248→        // Whitespace: space, tab
   249→        if ch == ' ' || ch == '\t' {
   250→            if in_word {
   251→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   252→                in_word = false;
   253→                last_emitted_op = None;
   254→            }
   255→            i += 1;
   256→            continue;
   257→        }
   258→
   259→        // Comment: # at start of token (not mid-word)
   260→        if ch == '#' && !in_word {
   261→            // Skip rest of line
   262→            while i < len && chars[i] != '\n' {
   263→                i += 1;
   264→            }
   265→            // Don't consume the \n — let it be processed as an operator
   266→            continue;
   267→        }
   268→
   269→        // Newline: command separator, continuation, or heredoc body start
   270→        if ch == '\n' {
   271→            if in_word {
   272→                tokens.push(Token::Word(std::mem::take(&mut word)));
   273→                in_word = false;
   274→                last_emitted_op = None;
   275→            }
   276→            // If heredoc delimiter is set but body hasn't started, enter body mode
   277→            if heredoc_delimiter.is_some() && !heredoc_in_body {
   278→                heredoc_in_body = true;
   279→                i += 1;
   280→                continue;
   281→            }
   282→            // After |, &&, || → continuation (skip newline)
   283→            let is_continuation = matches!(last_emitted_op, Some("|" | "&&" | "||"));
   284→            if !is_continuation {
   285→                last_emitted_op = Some("\n");
   286→                tokens.push(Token::Operator("\n".to_string()));
   287→            }
   288→            i += 1;
   289→            continue;
   290→        }
   291→
   292→        // Single quote
   293→        if ch == '\'' {
   294→            in_word = true;
   295→            i += 1;
   296→            while i < len && chars[i] != '\'' {
   297→                word.push(chars[i]);
   298→                i += 1;
   299→            }
   300→            if i >= len {
   301→                // Unmatched single quote — fail-open
   302→                return fail_open(cmd);
   303→            }
   304→            i += 1; // skip closing '
   305→            continue;
   306→        }
   307→
   308→        // Double quote
   309→        if ch == '"' {
   310→            in_word = true;
   311→            i += 1;
   312→            while i < len && chars[i] != '"' {
   313→                if chars[i] == '\\' && i + 1 < len {
   314→                    let next = chars[i + 1];
   315→                    match next {
   316→                        '"' | '\\' | '$' | '`' => {
   317→                            word.push(next);
   318→                            i += 2;
   319→                        }
   320→                        '\n' => {
   321→                            // Line continuation inside double quotes
   322→                            i += 2;
   323→                        }
   324→                        _ => {
   325→                            word.push('\\');
   326→                            word.push(next);
   327→                            i += 2;
   328→                        }
   329→                    }
   330→                    continue;
   331→                }
   332→                // $() inside double quotes — extract for recursive parsing
   333→                if chars[i] == '$' && i + 1 < len && chars[i + 1] == '(' {
   334→                    let start = i;
   335→                    i += 2;
   336→                    match find_matching_close_paren(&chars, i) {
   337→                        Some((inner, new_i)) => {
   338→                            i = new_i;
   339→                            let subst_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   340→                            word.push_str(&subst_text);
   341→                            if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   342→                                let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   343→                                if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   344→                                    inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   345→                                }
   346→                            }
   347→                        }
   348→                        None => return fail_open(cmd),
   349→                    }
   350→                    continue;
   351→                }
   352→                // Backtick inside double quotes
   353→                if chars[i] == '`' {
   354→                    let start = i;
   355→                    i += 1;
   356→                    let mut inner = String::new();
   357→                    while i < len && chars[i] != '`' {
   358→                        inner.push(chars[i]);
   359→                        i += 1;
   360→                    }
   361→                    if i >= len {
   362→                        return fail_open(cmd);
   363→                    }
   364→                    i += 1; // skip closing `
   365→                    let bt_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   366→                    word.push_str(&bt_text);
   367→                    if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   368→                        let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   369→                        if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   370→                            inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   371→                        }
   372→                    }
   373→                    continue;
   374→                }
   375→                word.push(chars[i]);
   376→                i += 1;
   377→            }
   378→            if i >= len {
   379→                return fail_open(cmd);
   380→            }
   381→            i += 1; // skip closing "
   382→            continue;
   383→        }
   384→
   385→        // Backslash outside quotes
   386→        if ch == '\\' {
   387→            if i + 1 >= len {
   388→                // Dangling backslash — fail-open
   389→                return fail_open(cmd);
   390→            }
   391→            let next = chars[i + 1];
   392→            if next == '\n' {
   393→                // Line continuation
   394→                i += 2;
   395→                continue;
   396→            }
   397→            in_word = true;
   398→            word.push(next);
   399→            i += 2;
   400→            continue;
   401→        }
   402→
   403→        // $() command substitution outside quotes
   404→        if ch == '$' && i + 1 < len && chars[i + 1] == '(' {
   405→            let start = i;
   406→            i += 2;
   407→            match find_matching_close_paren(&chars, i) {
   408→                Some((inner, new_i)) => {
   409→                    i = new_i;
   410→                    let subst_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   411→                    in_word = true;
   412→                    word.push_str(&subst_text);
   413→                    if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   414→                        let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   415→                        if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   416→                            inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   417→                        }
   418→                    }
   419→                }
   420→                None => return fail_open(cmd),
   421→            }
   422→            continue;
   423→        }
   424→
   425→        // Backtick command substitution outside quotes
   426→        if ch == '`' {
   427→            let start = i;
   428→            i += 1;
   429→            let mut inner = String::new();
   430→            while i < len && chars[i] != '`' {
   431→                inner.push(chars[i]);
   432→                i += 1;
   433→            }
   434→            if i >= len {
   435→                return fail_open(cmd);
   436→            }
   437→            i += 1; // skip closing `
   438→            let bt_text: String = chars[start..i].iter().collect();
   439→            in_word = true;
   440→            word.push_str(&bt_text);
   441→            if depth < MAX_SUBST_DEPTH {
   442→                let inner_segs = parse_segments_inner(&inner, depth + 1);
   443→                if !inner_segs.is_empty() {
   444→                    inner_segment_groups.push(inner_segs);
   445→                }
   446→            }
   447→            continue;
   448→        }
   449→
   450→        // Operator matching: greedy longest-first, table-driven.
   451→        // Ordered longest-first so ">>" matches before ">", "&&" before "&", etc.
   452→        // This is the same algorithm bash uses (shellmeta + peek-ahead).
   453→        //
   454→        // NOT in this table (require special handling):
   455→        //   <<  — heredoc (consumes delimiter + body)
   456→        //   <   — must come AFTER heredoc check (< vs << ambiguity)
   457→        //   <&  — must come AFTER heredoc check
   458→        //   <>  — must come AFTER heredoc check
   459→        //   \n  — newline continuation logic
   460→        const SIMPLE_OPS: &[&str] = &[
   461→            "&>>", "&&", "&>", ">>", ">&", ">|", "||", ">", "&", "|", ";", "(", ")",
   462→        ];
   463→        if let Some(op) = SIMPLE_OPS.iter().find(|op| {
   464→            let ob = op.as_bytes();
   465→            i + ob.len() <= len
   466→                && ob
   467→                    .iter()
   468→                    .enumerate()
   469→                    .all(|(j, &b)| chars[i + j] == b as char)
   470→        }) {
   471→            emit_op!(op);
   472→            i += op.len();
   473→            continue;

[assistant] **31 commits, 5109 lines, 535 unit tests, 68 integration tests, 0 failures, 0 clippy warnings.**

The tokenizer now has no remaining edge cases or duplicated logic:

| Component | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `$()` handling | Duplicated (inside-dquotes + outside) | `parse_dollar_subst()` — one method, called from both |
| Backtick handling | Duplicated (inside-dquotes + outside) | `parse_backtick()` — one method, called from both |
| `<` family | Separate explicit handler after heredoc | In `SIMPLE_OPS` table — heredoc runs first, `<` family matches in table |
| Operator emission | `emit_op!` macro (captured mutable state) | `emit_op()` method on struct (clean `&mut self`) |
| Word flushing | Repeated `if in_word { ... }` in 4+ places | `emit_word()` method |
| State | 8 local variables | `Tokenizer<'a>` struct with 13 fields |

Special cases remaining (justified):
- `<<` heredoc: fundamentally different (consumes delimiter + multi-line body). Must run before table to avoid `<` ambiguity.
- `\n` newline: continuation logic + heredoc body entry. Interacts with `last_emitted_op` state in ways table matching can't express.

Everything else is table-driven or method-based.

     3→/// Read all of stdin. Returns Err on OS-level infrastructure errors (ENXIO, EAGAIN, ENOENT)
     4→/// so the caller can fail-open. Other IO errors are returned as-is.
     5→pub fn read_stdin() -> io::Result<String> {
     6→    let mut buf = String::new();
     7→    io::stdin().lock().read_to_string(&mut buf)?;
     8→    Ok(buf)
     9→}
    10→
    11→/// Returns true for OS error codes that indicate stdin is unavailable due to infrastructure
    12→/// issues (subagent context, background process, missing FD). Callers should fail-open.
    13→pub fn is_infra_error(e: &io::Error) -> bool {
    14→    matches!(
    15→        e.raw_os_error(),
    16→        Some(6) | Some(11) | Some(2) // ENXIO=6, EAGAIN=11, ENOENT=2
    17→    )
    18→}
