[assistant] Let me write up the initial proposal to the plan file so you can see it and we can refine from there.

[tool] Write({"file_path":"/home/node/.claude/plans/hidden-discovering-brooks.md","content":"# Operational Infrastructure: POC → Production\n\n## Context\n\nThe guard system and E2E test framework have production-grade code quality (545 unit tests, 68 integration tests, 5 code review rounds, POSIX-aligned tokenizer). But the operational infrastructure around them is POC-level — no staleness detection, no automated cleanup, no observability, no CI parity. This plan addresses every gap needed to run multi-agent development reliably.\n\n## Current State\n\n| Area | What exists | What's missing |\n|---|---|---|\n| Guard binary | Shared at /workspace/target/release/guard. Auto-build on Docker start. Auto-rebuild in verify.sh when guard source changes. | Staleness detection. Version verification. CI build. Health check. Rollback. |\n| Worktree lifecycle | 5-step manual cleanup in CLAUDE.md. worktree-rebase.sh. lib/worktree.sh utilities. launch-phase.sh orchestration. | Single cleanup script. Orphan detection. Auto-cleanup. |\n| E2E tests | Attestation workflow. Force flag. Concurrency group. Sheet reset at run start. Timestamped room IDs. | Post-failure cleanup. Run-scoped test data. Rate limiting. Ephemeral sheets. |\n| Observability | Guard block messages shown to agents. | Structured decision logging. Metrics. Block rate alerting. Agent activity dashboard. |\n| Config management | settings.json with hooks. SKILL.md decision table. | settings.json validation. Decision table drift detection. |\n\n---\n\n## P0 — Blocks Production Use\n\n### P0.1: Guard Binary Staleness Detection + Auto-Rebuild\n\n**Problem**: When main merges a guard change, all worktrees run the old binary. We hit this during the guard refactor — agents enforced old rules after new code merged.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. `crates/guard/build.rs` — embed SHA-256 of source files at compile time via `env!(\"GUARD_SOURCE_HASH\")`\n2. `guard --version` subcommand in main.rs — prints the embedded hash\n3. `scripts/guard-check.sh` wrapper — computes current source hash, compares to `guard --version`, rebuilds if mismatched (with file lock to prevent parallel builds)\n4. `.claude/settings.json` — update hook commands to call wrapper instead of binary directly\n5. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — add `cargo build --release -p guard` + `cargo test -p guard`\n\n**Files**: `crates/guard/build.rs` (new), `crates/guard/src/main.rs`, `scripts/guard-check.sh` (new), `.claude/settings.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`\n\n**Verification**: Modify a comment in lib.rs → guard-check.sh detects mismatch → rebuilds → new hash matches.\n\n---\n\n### P0.2: Guard Health Check (Selftest)\n\n**Problem**: No way to verify the binary works after rebuild. Bad guard change = every agent stuck or unprotected.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. `guard selftest` subcommand — runs hardcoded canonical inputs through check_bash/check_edit, prints pass/fail JSON\n2. `guard-check.sh` calls selftest after every rebuild — if selftest fails, keeps old binary (rollback)\n3. CI runs selftest after build\n\n**Files**: `crates/guard/src/main.rs`, `crates/guard/src/lib.rs` (add `pub fn selftest()`), `scripts/guard-check.sh`\n\n**Verification**: Introduce deliberate regression → build → selftest fails → old binary preserved.\n\n---\n\n### P0.3: settings.json Validation\n\n**Problem**: Malformed settings.json silently breaks all hooks.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. `scripts/validate-settings.sh` — parse JSON, check required keys, validate referenced binaries exist\n2. Add to pre-commit hook, CI, and full-verify.sh\n\n**Files**: `scripts/validate-settings.sh` (new), `scripts/pre-commit-hook.sh`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `scripts/full-verify.sh`\n\n**Verification**: Introduce syntax error → pre-commit blocks. Remove PreToolUse key → validator fails.\n\n---\n\n## P1 — Degrades Production Use\n\n### P1.1: Worktree Cleanup Script\n\n**Problem**: 5-step manual cleanup with squash-merge conditional logic. Agents get stuck.\n\n**Approach**:\n```\nscripts/worktree-cleanup.sh <worktree-path> [--pr <number>]\n```\nAutomates: verify merge → delete remote branch → squash-merge fast-forward → remove worktree → prune. Sources `scripts/lib/worktree.sh` for `delete_merged_branch`. Runs from /workspace, not from within the worktree.\n\n**Files**: `scripts/worktree-cleanup.sh` (new), `.claude/worktrees/CLAUDE.md`\n\n---\n\n### P1.2: Orphan Worktree Detection\n\n**Problem**: 19 stale worktrees from previous sessions. No way to identify which are safe to remove.\n\n**Approach**:\n```\nscripts/worktree-status.sh\n```\nFor each worktree: get branch → find PR via `gh pr list --head` → check PR state → print table with recommendations (STALE/ACTIVE/ORPHAN/LOCAL_ONLY).\n\n**Files**: `scripts/worktree-status.sh` (new)\n\n---\n\n### P1.3: Guard Structured Logging\n\n**Problem**: No visibility into guard decisions. Can't audit, can't detect false positive spikes.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. After every decision in main.rs, append JSONL to `$GUARD_LOG` (default `.claude/logs/guard-decisions.jsonl`)\n2. `scripts/guard-metrics.sh` — reads JSONL, prints total decisions, block rate, top reasons\n3. Logging failures are silently ignored (guard must never block on logging)\n\n**Files**: `crates/guard/src/main.rs`, `scripts/guard-metrics.sh` (new), `.gitignore`\n\n---\n\n### P1.4: E2E Test Data Isolation\n\n**Problem**: Crashed runs leave stale data. Concurrent runs can interfere.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. Run-ID scoped test data — prefix seed task IDs with `e2e-{timestamp}-`\n2. Global teardown resets sheet after every run (even on failure)\n3. Cleanup sweep in global setup removes rows with IDs older than 1 hour\n4. Rate-limited fetch wrapper with exponential backoff on 429s\n\n**Files**: `e2e/global-teardown.ts`, `e2e/helpers/sheet-lifecycle.ts`, `e2e/helpers/rate-limited-fetch.ts` (new), `playwright.config.ts`\n\n---\n\n### P1.5: Decision Table Drift Detection\n\n**Problem**: SKILL.md decision table can drift from code. Agents rely on docs.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. `crates/guard/tests/decision-table.json` — every row from SKILL.md as a test case\n2. `crates/guard/tests/decision_table.rs` — data-driven integration test\n3. `cargo test -p guard` automatically catches drift\n\n**Files**: `crates/guard/tests/decision-table.json` (new), `crates/guard/tests/decision_table.rs` (new), `.claude/skills/hooks/SKILL.md`\n\n---\n\n## P2 — Operational Convenience\n\n### P2.1: Guard Rollback via Git Ref\n\n`guard-check.sh --rollback <git-ref>` — checkout guard source from ref, build, selftest, replace binary if passes.\n\n### P2.2: Agent Activity Dashboard\n\n`scripts/agent-dashboard.sh` — aggregate worktree status, guard logs, recent commits per worktree into one table.\n\n### P2.3: Worktree Auto-Cleanup\n\n`scripts/worktree-cleanup.sh` called from launch-phase.sh `--auto-cleanup` flag after pipeline completion.\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Order\n\n```\nPhase 1 (Foundation):     P0.1 + P0.3  (parallel — guard staleness + settings validation)\nPhase 2 (Safety):         P0.2 + P1.5  (guard selftest + decision table drift)\nPhase 3 (Lifecycle):      P1.1 + P1.2  (cleanup script + orphan detection)\nPhase 4 (Observability):  P1.3 + P1.4  (guard logging + E2E isolation)\nPhase 5 (Convenience):    P2.1 + P2.2 + P2.3  (rollback, dashboard, auto-cleanup)\n```\n\nEach phase is one PR. Phases 1-2 are prerequisites. Phases 3-4 can be parallel.\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. Should guard-check.sh rebuild trigger on every hook invocation (~100ms overhead) or only once per session?\n2. Should structured logging default to on or opt-in via env var?\n3. Should worktree-status.sh be integrated into the guard itself or stay as a standalone script?\n4. Should decision-table.json be auto-generated from SKILL.md or manually maintained?\n"})

Can't audit, can't detect false positive spikes.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. After every decision in main.rs, append JSONL to `$GUARD_LOG` (default `.claude/logs/guard-decisions.jsonl`)\n2. `scripts/guard-metrics.sh` — reads JSONL, prints total decisions, block rate, top reasons\n3. Logging failures are silently ignored (guard must never block on logging)\n\n**Files**: `crates/guard/src/main.rs`, `scripts/guard-metrics.sh` (new), `.gitignore`\n\n---\n\n### P1.4: E2E Test Data Isolation\n\n**Problem**: Crashed runs leave stale data. Concurrent runs can interfere.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. Run-ID scoped test data — prefix seed task IDs with `e2e-{timestamp}-`\n2. Global teardown resets sheet after every run (even on failure)\n3. Cleanup sweep in global setup removes rows with IDs older than 1 hour\n4. Rate-limited fetch wrapper with exponential backoff on 429s\n\n**Files**: `e2e/global-teardown.ts`, `e2e/helpers/sheet-lifecycle.ts`, `e2e/helpers/rate-limited-fetch.ts` (new), `playwright.config.ts`\n\n---\n\n### P1.5: Decision Table Drift Detection\n\n**Problem**: SKILL.md decision table can drift from code. Agents rely on docs.\n\n**Approach**:\n1. `crates/guard/tests/decision-table.json` — every row from SKILL.md as a test case\n2. `crates/guard/tests/decision_table.rs` — data-driven integration test\n3. `cargo test -p guard` automatically catches drift\n\n**Files**: `crates/guard/tests/decision-table.json` (new), `crates/guard/tests/decision_table.rs` (new), `.claude/skills/hooks/SKILL.md`\n\n---\n\n## P2 — Operational Convenience\n\n### P2.1: Guard Rollback via Git Ref\n\n`guard-check.sh --rollback <git-ref>` — checkout guard source from ref, build, selftest, replace binary if passes.\n\n### P2.2: Agent Activity Dashboard\n\n`scripts/agent-dashboard.sh` — aggregate worktree status, guard logs, recent commits per worktree into one table.\n\n### P2.3: Worktree Auto-Cleanup\n\n`scripts/worktree-cleanup.sh` called from launch-phase.sh `--auto-cleanup` flag after pipeline completion.\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Order\n\n```\nPhase 1 (Foundation):     P0.1 + P0.3  (parallel — guard staleness + settings validation)\nPhase 2 (Safety):         P0.2 + P1.5  (guard selftest + decision table drift)\nPhase 3 (Lifecycle):      P1.1 + P1.2  (cleanup script + orphan detection)\nPhase 4 (Observability):  P1.3 + P1.4  (guard logging + E2E isolation)\nPhase 5 (Convenience):    P2.1 + P2.2 + P2.3  (rollback, dashboard, auto-cleanup)\n```\n\nEach phase is one PR. Phases 1-2 are prerequisites. Phases 3-4 can be parallel.\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. Should guard-check.sh rebuild trigger on every hook invocation (~100ms overhead) or only once per session?\n2. Should structured logging default to on or opt-in via env var?\n3. Should worktree-status.sh be integrated into the guard itself or stay as a standalone script?\n4. Should decision-table.json be auto-generated from SKILL.md or manually maintained?\n","planFilePath":"/home/node/.claude/plans/hidden-discovering-brooks.md"})

```
The real question is: how does the guard learn to protect two projects?

  Right now the guard hardcodes /workspace/ as the protected root. That's why it works for
   ganttlet. For it to also protect unreel, it needs to become project-root-aware.

  The guard change needed (regardless of where unreel lives)

  Replace hardcoded /workspace/ with detected project root — the nearest .git ancestor
  from CWD:

  Current:  is_protected_path() checks "is this under /workspace/?"
  Future:   is_protected_path() checks "is this under the project root?"
            where project root = git rev-parse --show-toplevel (or Rust equivalent)

  This is a moderate refactor but it makes every rule work for ANY project:
  - "Don't edit on main" → checks the current project's main, not hardcoded /workspace
  - "Use worktrees" → worktrees relative to current project root
  - "Don't push to main" → checks current project's remote
  - CWD-dependent rules → relative to detected project root

  Where unreel should live given this

  /workspace/unreel/ (Option A). Here's why:

  1. One guard binary protects both. Once the guard is project-root-aware, an agent with
  CWD in /workspace/unreel/ gets unreel's rules. An agent with CWD in /workspace/ gets
  ganttlet's rules. The guard detects the project root from CWD and applies rules relative
   to that root.
  2. Cross-reference stays easy. Unreel agents read ganttlet at ../../src/ (read is never
  blocked). POC workflows at ../../scripts/sdk/.
  3. Nested repo is invisible to ganttlet. /workspace/.gitignore has unreel/ — ganttlet's
  git doesn't know it exists. The guard sees two separate .git roots and treats them
  independently.
  4. Worktrees work naturally. Unreel gets its own worktrees at
  /workspace/unreel/.claude/worktrees/. The guard protects them with the same discipline
  as ganttlet worktrees but scoped to unreel's root.
  5. Guard becomes reusable. The refactor makes the guard work for any project, anywhere.
  This is the first step toward extracting it as its own distributable tool — which is
  what you'll want when unreel ships its own guard-like policy system.

  Implementation path

  The guard refactor is small enough to do now:

  1. Replace is_workspace_cwd() → is_project_root_cwd() (detect from .git)
  2. Replace hardcoded /workspace/ in is_protected_path() → detected project root
  3. Worktree paths become {project_root}/.claude/worktrees/ instead of
  /workspace/.claude/worktrees/
  4. Test with both CWDs: /workspace/ and /workspace/unreel/

  The logic doesn't change — just the reference point. All 545 existing tests pass with
  the substitution since they're relative, not absolute.

  Want me to do the guard refactor first, then set up unreel? Or set up unreel first and
  do the guard refactor as a follow-up?
```
I'm not looking for you to solve the specific problem, but it does have me wondering about a few things
1: how do we support monorepos? Would these fixes help?
2: we probably need to make the guard into it's own project so it can be used to protect all of my agent-heavy projects going forward
3: if this is protecting several project, we may need to make enforcement more flexible. I'm thinking about how eslint allows rule configuration with easy defaults to serve a broader set of users.
